findings.description: DOME-DECIDER SCHEDULE NOT RESTORED: The actual scheduled task cron for dome-decider is */30 (every 30 minutes, Opus). Priority-queue.json schedule_state says decider_current_interval_minutes: 240, and the decider's own schedule_check reports 'All agents at BAU intervals. No mismatches detected.' But the ACTUAL cron was never changed. Root cause: decider.md Step E4 says 'Do NOT call update_scheduled_task — log schedule_action_needed' but the decider checks priority-queue.json metadata (which it already set to 240) rather than calling list_scheduled_tasks to verify the real cron. So it declares 'ok' when the task is still fast. The analyst (restored to 2h) and curmudgeon (restored to 4h) were correctly updated at some point, but the decider's own schedule was not. Result: 12 decider reports in the last 24 hours. Of the 6 sampled, 4 had 0 patches and 0 issues created — ~67% no-op rate at an Opus model. Estimated waste: ~8x BAU Opus budget for the decider agent since 2026-04-11T10:42Z.
findings.evidence: list_scheduled_tasks: dome-decider cronExpression=*/30, description still says 'Every 30min (churn-and-burn: 68 pending digest items)'. priority-queue.json schedule_state.decider_current_interval_minutes=240, mode='bau', mode_set_at='2026-04-11T10:42:23.359Z'. Decider report 2026-04-12T09-11: schedule_check.status='ok', schedule_check.note='All agents at BAU intervals'. 12 decider daily-report files in last 24h.
findings.suggested_fix: IMMEDIATE HUMAN ACTION: Update dome-decider scheduled task cron from '*/30 * * * *' to '0 */4 * * *' (every 4h) and update description to remove churn-and-burn reference. Also fix decider.md Step E4 to cross-check actual scheduled task crons via list_scheduled_tasks instead of trusting priority-queue.json metadata.

findings.description: CLAUDE.MD GREW 39% (156L → 217L): Exceeds the 15% major alert threshold. Since every agent reads CLAUDE.md, this growth multiplies across all 8 agents. The growth is 61 lines. Need to identify what was added and whether any of it could be extracted to reference files or removed.
findings.evidence: wc -l CLAUDE.md = 217. Previous tinker report (2026-04-11) measured 156L. Delta = +61 lines (+39%).
findings.suggested_fix: Audit the new 61 lines. If they are agent-specific content (e.g., new pipeline documentation, expanded file ownership entries), consider extracting to reference files. If they are corrections to existing content, they may be justified. File an issue for the decider to review and trim.

findings.evidence: decider.md line 12: '(every 4h)', line 14: '(every 10min)'. Actual crons: poller '0 */12', curmudgeon '0 */4'.

findings.description: OWNERSHIP MISALIGNMENT PERSISTS: 4 files listed as git-owned in CLAUDE.md and workspace-sync.md but MISSING from build.js OWNERSHIP: data/predictions.json, docs/llms.txt, docs/sitemap.xml, docs/robots.txt. Additionally, data/kill-shots.json, monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/, monitor/external-reports/ remain unclassified. Flagged in 2 consecutive reports.
findings.evidence: grep for each file in build.js returns no match.
findings.suggested_fix: Route through decider to add to build.js OWNERSHIP.

findings.description: DECIDER STEP E4 DESIGN FLAW — METADATA VS REALITY: The decider checks priority-queue.json schedule_state (which it writes itself) to verify 'no schedule mismatches.' This is circular — it always passes because the decider already set the metadata to BAU. The check should call list_scheduled_tasks and compare actual cron expressions against expected BAU values. This flaw is why the decider's own 30-min cron was invisible for 24+ hours.
findings.evidence: decider.md lines 212-222: Step E4 checks priority-queue.json schedule_state. Decider report schedule_check.status='ok' while actual cron is */30.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to decider.md Step E4: 'Cross-check by calling list_scheduled_tasks for dome-analyst, dome-curmudgeon, dome-decider. Compare actual cronExpression against BAU expectations (analyst: 0 */2, curmudgeon: 0 */4, decider: 0 */4). If any mismatch, log schedule_action_needed with the specific task and expected cron.'

OPERATOR-RUNBOOK.md (50L) and VERSION-ARCHIVE.md (27L) are new small additions.

previous_followup.evidence: Analyst and curmudgeon were correctly restored to BAU crons (2h and 4h respectively — verified via list_scheduled_tasks). However, the decider's own cron remains at */30. The priority-queue.json now tracks all three agents' schedule_state and the mode is 'bau', but the decider never actually called update_scheduled_task on itself. The design says 'Do NOT call update_scheduled_task' and instead log schedule_action_needed, but the decider's schedule_check logic uses metadata instead of actual crons, so it never flags the mismatch.

recommendations.description: URGENT: Restore dome-decider schedule from '*/30 * * * *' to '0 */4 * * *'. Update description to 'Triage dome model issues, process backlog. Every 4h (BAU).' The decider has been running at churn-and-burn cadence for 24+ hours since BAU restore, burning ~42 unnecessary Opus invocations. This is the single highest-cost waste in the pipeline right now.

FIX decider.md Step E4 schedule verification: Replace metadata-based check with actual list_scheduled_tasks cross-check. Current design checks priority-queue.json schedule_state (which the decider itself writes) — circular and guaranteed to pass. The fix: Step E4 should call list_scheduled_tasks, extract actual cron expressions for dome-analyst/curmudgeon/decider, and compare against expected BAU values. Log schedule_action_needed if ANY mismatch.

AUDIT CLAUDE.md 39% growth (156→217L). Identify the 61 added lines, determine if any are agent-specific content that could move to reference files. Every line in CLAUDE.md costs 8x (one per agent). This is the highest-leverage bloat reduction target.
Apply PROP-005 (decider pre-flight work check). 6th consecutive report. Would save ~700 lines of reference reads per no-op run. PROP file has exact find/replace text.
Fix decider.md stale schedule descriptions (lines 12-14). Poller: 4h→12h. Curmudgeon: 10min→4h BAU/30min churn. 2nd consecutive report flagging.
Fix OWNERSHIP misalignment: add 4+3 files to build.js OWNERSHIP. Routes through decider since build.js is git-owned.

tinker_self_audit.self_assessment: Substantive run. Critical finding: decider schedule stuck at 30min for 24h was invisible to the decider itself due to Step E4 design flaw. This is exactly the kind of cross-agent verification the tinker exists to do. Also set context load baseline for future trend tracking.

notes_to_future_tinker: The decider schedule restoration is Priority 1 — if not fixed by next run, manually verify by checking list_scheduled_tasks output. The CLAUDE.md 39% growth needs a line-by-line audit to understand what was added (diff against git history). PROP-005 is now at 6 reports — consider bundling it with the Step E4 fix and schedule description fix into a single PROP-007 'decider hygiene' proposal to reduce implementation friction. The reference growth (18%) is mostly from the analyst-mode5-prospective feature — legitimate but watch for continued creep.
